summary: The new Android 16 testing libraries (`3.7.0-alpha02`) were compiled by Google using Kotlin 2.1.0. Because Kotlin metadata is backwards-compatible but not forward-compatible, your current Kotlin compiler (1.9.24) cannot read the new libraries. To fix this, we need to upgrade the project to Kotlin 2.1.0. This is actually a great architectural upgrade: as of Kotlin 2.0, the Jetpack Compose compiler is natively built into Kotlin itself, making it much faster and more stable.
